candidates.stmt:
Sharmila has lived in Crawley for 18 years, raising her family in the town and working her way up from working in Boots to running a successful transport business for Special Needs children.

She has been active in the community for many years, helping as a voluntary language teacher at Hazelwick School, providing patient transport, supporting Run Gatwick and teaching Indian and Classical dance.

Sharmila wants to be your councillor to build on her history of giving back to the community by providing a strong voice for Bewbush and North Broadfield.

Ryan has lived in the area for 3 years. They are committed to strengthening the local community, promoting sustainability, and ensuring that decisions made at council level reflect the needs of residents.

Professionally, they work as a machinist. Their experience includes:
Precision engineering and problem-solving in a manufacturing environment
Working with technical drawings and maintaining high standards of accuracy and quality
A practical, hands-on understanding of industry, sustainability, and responsible resource use.

“I’m standing because I don’t like seeing how divided people have become. I believe we can do better as a community. I want to focus on practical improvements like properly fixing our roads instead of repeating the same repairs and making solar energy more affordable while helping bring people together in a more positive, respectful way.”

I have lived in Bewbush with my family since 1996. This is a great part of the Town to live in. We are a real community and yet politicians take our neighbourhood for granted. I know how important local facilities are to local people and so much has been given up in recent years, without our politicians putting up a fight on our behalf. We have had to stand up for ourselves when they are supposed to stand up for us. We have lost our public toilets, our open spaces are not what they were, and now they are trying to take our children’s adventure playgrounds away.  Our own councillors made no objection to these things. We are led to believe there is no money and yet there seems to be plenty for councils to splash around for things that we don’t have any say about until it is too late - like the superfast broadband that most of us don’t need and can’t afford anyway; like the Kingsgate carpark bought on a whim that now needs loads of work doing to it, and that we will have to pay over the odds to use. It has been reported today that £1 million plus has recently been given over to developing council land at Metcalf way, and we aren’t even given a clue what it is for. This is our money, and it is not good enough. If you elect me, I will represent your interests – not other shadowy interests that nobody is allowed to know anything about until it is too late. I have always stood up for ordinary people and I always will.

Tim has represented Broadfield on Crawley Borough Council since 2014, helping to deliver investment in Broadfield Barton and local play areas, campaigning against litter with Talk Broadfield, and working to improve the quality of local housing associations’ services.

Tim understands the difficulties many families in Broadfield are facing right now due to COVID and saw his own job at Virgin Atlantic disappear due to the pandemic. On the council, Tim has been chair of both the Governance Committee and the Budget Advisory Group.

Tim grew up in Crawley and returned to the area with his family after completing his studies. He has lived in Furnace Green for the past 4 years. Proud to be a part of the local community and wants to ensure that decisions made at council level reflect the needs of Furnace Green residents.
Professionally, he works as a regional manager for the South East within the Horizon Group. Their experience includes:
● Project management.
● Employee welfare.
● Business liaison.

“Furnace Green is a great place to live. It has lots of outdoor spaces, strong transport links and most of all a real diverse community. I see the strength of this community and want to be a voice to support any investment from the council to make the services and facilities in Furnace Green even better for those who live here.”

Jilly has lived in Ifield for decades, raising her family in the neighbourhood, and representing the community on Crawley Borough Council for several years. Community is very important to Jilly and for many years she has been active in local voluntary groups, such as the Royal Naval Association.

As a resident, she is deeply concerned with the proposals for West of Ifield and has been fighting alongside other Crawley Labour representatives to stop the Government’s developer from ruining the local countryside.

Ines Manning has lived in Ifield for 28 years and is now moved to stand and become our Green councillor out of frustration at the standard of local amenities. Ines would speak up at council and argue to provide free toilets at the shops, playgrounds for children, electric car charge points and helping to end the traffic misery along the Rusper Road. It is a bottleneck made worse by narrow pavements and it is unsafe at the junction with Tangmere. As it is the only road out of Ifield West it gets very busy and pedestrians and cyclists face frustrated drivers. Without commitment from elected councillors this can only get worse, especially if more houses are built.

Tahira is passionate about helping people and doing what she can to help make a difference to the town, having previously served Crawley as a councillor and Deputy Mayor.

She has lived in Crawley for almost her entire life, working in the town and raising her family here.

Tahira understands the problems facing many families in Langley Green and Tushmore right now due to COVID, having previously lost a job due to cuts at the airport. Since then she has played a role helping to end the pandemic, working for the NHS on tracking and tracing.

Laura has lived in Crawley for 14 years. She is committed to strengthening the local community and ensuring that decisions made at council level reflect the needs of residents.
Professionally, she works as a customer service executive. Her experience includes:
1.Supporting vulnerable people.
2.Showing empathy and listening to everyone’s voices.
3.Volunteering for St Catherine’s Hospice.

“I’m standing as a candidate because I’m committed to help bring in better change for our society. To make the best and most impactful changes, I will always keep in mind my community’s day-to-day challenges and respect everyone equally.”

Hello, I’m Kemuel Thompson, and I’m proud to be standing as your Liberal Democrat candidate for Langley Green & Tushmore (and Ifield East) in the upcoming election.

I’m standing because I believe our community deserves a strong, positive voice—one that listens, acts, and delivers real results. As a young candidate, I bring fresh energy, new ideas, and a determination to make local politics work better for everyone.

I’ve lived in Crawley all my life, attended local schools, and worked in the area, so I understand first-hand the issues that matter most to our community.

The Liberal Democrats stand for fairness, opportunity, and strong local communities. I share those values, and I’m committed to putting them into action here in Crawley.

My priorities for Langley Green & Tushmore are:
• Stronger local services – ensuring our streets are clean, safe, and well-maintained
• Affordable housing – pushing for more genuinely affordable homes for local people
• Protecting our environment – improving green spaces and tackling pollution
• Supporting young people – creating better opportunities, skills, and community support
• Listening to residents – being visible, accessible, and accountable all year round

I believe politics should be about people—not parties. That means working hard, listening to your concerns, and standing up for our community at every opportunity.

This election is your chance to choose a representative who will speak up for you and work tirelessly on your behalf. If elected, I will always put Langley Green & Tushmore first.

Thank you for your support.

Kemuel Thompson

Liberal Democrat Candidate for Langley Green & Tushmore (and Ifield East)

Max has lived/worked in Crawley for most of his life. He is committed to strengthening the local community and ensuring that decisions made at council level reflect the needs of residents.
Professionally, he works as a Financial Systems Manager at the United Bible Societies and is a committed Christian.
His experience includes:
Committee member of the local Church Parish and previous treasurer.
Much of Max’s career has been spent in the aviation industry and working at Gatwick,
He was involved in the successful opposition to the Center Parcs development just south of Maidenbower.

“Crawley has always been my home, and I have come to know and appreciate so many people who live here. I am keen to give back to the community.”

Nick has lived and worked in Crawley all his life and has family and community ties of many generations. He has the commitment to ensure the needs of residents at council level. Professionally Nick works as an EV technician. Nick’s mother worked as a physiotherapist at Crawley hospital giving Nick a great respect for the vital service of the NHS. Crawley hospital has had many services cut or reduced over the years and one of Nick’s main focus points is returning our urgent treatment centre to 24-hour operation along with pressure to increase NHS spending.

Neal Murdoch is 49 years old and has lived in Crawley all of his life. He works as a business coordinator at Equiniti Paymaster in Three Bridges. He has been involved in local politics since the age of 15 with both of his parents serving as borough councillors on Crawley Borough Council.

“I am standing to give an alternative voice onto the council and to give a voice to those who sometimes are overlooked and ignored.”

Heidi James-Dunbar has lived in Worth for nine years. She is committed to strengthening the local community, working with the community to create a brighter future  and ensuring that decisions made at council level reflect the needs of all residents.
Professionally, she works as an academic and writer in education. Her experience includes:
Academic Faculty Director – her work in education (of over 20 years) has included safeguarding of all users, curriculum design and planning, staff management, student development and research. Fundamental to her role was ensuring students from all backgrounds, learning abilities and styles were supported and included in the curriculum offering, and as part of her initiative she worked on decolonising the curriculum and published a number of papers in international journals sharing findings and processes with peers. She is a published author (both academic and fiction).
Vice-Chair of Governors for Maidenbower Infant School (Local Authority governor responsible for SEND).

“I’m standing because I feel lucky to live here and while things are really difficult at the moment for many of us, I know that by coming together we can create a safer, secure and brighter community where we can all flourish.”

My career has been in software engineering. I've worked in events media, in finance and currently in defence where I manage a project, architect the solutions and write the code. I've spent the last 20 years facing complex problems and solving them. I have been living in Crawley since 2016 and lived in Surrey before that.

I voted Leave in the 2016 referendum. I've never regretted that vote nor the following times we had to hammer the point home to our political class. Like so many others I voted for Boris in the hope that we had an optimistic, British patriot who would champion our people, our country and our values and reverse the downward trends we all experienced. Like so many others I have now seen what the Conservative party has become.

I will never give up on Britain, her values, people and history. With a strong reform of our institutions, we will save Britain.

My priorities are focussed on public safety, the safety of women and girls and to stop the rampant crime that is happening through Crawley.  Spending is out of control and that needs to be brought back to sensible levels and only on what benefits the good people of Crawley.  There are a myriad of other issues to resolve as well, such as restoring the high street, get council house repairs done as close to first time as possible, stop West of Ifield expansion and resist the illegal immigrant settlement into our communities putting our safety at great risk.  None of us at Reform are single issue, we hear all perspectives and record all problems raised to us for addressing.

Oh, and fix the damn potholes.

Zack Ali got married in 2009 and made Crawley his home. Since then, he has been a resident of Broadfield. Zack is a proud father of two young boys, both of whom went to Hilltop Primary School in Southgate.  He has received an honours degree in Business management and finance from University of Surrey.

In 2007 he worked for Domino’s Pizza and quickly ended up managing several stores across Sussex and Surrey. He was based at the store on Crawley High Street and led a team of store managers from across the region and was responsible for more than 200 staff. The Crawley store became one of the top 10 performing stores worldwide, whilst under Zack’s leadership. He went on to start his own business, this grew it to the point where it employed over 50 people and served thousands of customers every year.

Zack was elected to Crawley Borough Council and West Sussex County Council in 2021, representing Southgate and Gossops Green division of Crawley. He has sat on several committees and is a member of the shadow cabinet for Planning and Economic development on Crawley Borough Council.

He has been actively involved in Crawley politics since 2007, having worked alongside Henry Smith MP on various local issues and campaigns. Zack has also played a key role in several general election campaigns supporting Henry Smith MP to win the Crawley seat.

He has worked with a number local and national charities in support of a range charitable causes and issues. Including during the pandemic, where he gathered several likeminded people to set up a group to support vulnerable local residents. This group did grocery and food shopping for shielding families, vulnerable households and those on low incomes.

Zack also joined the NHS Volunteer Responders and helped local communities across Crawley to stay safe and feel supported. Taking part in activities such as welfare phone calls with residents living alone and dropping of medication and care packages.

Robin has lived in Crawley for fifty-four years. He is committed to strengthening the local community and ensuring that decisions made at council level reflect the needs of the people who actually live here.

Professionally, Robin works as a Head of Technology, leading teams to build things that serve people rather than impress them. His experience includes:

Leading travel and holiday technology teams for over a decade, making complex systems into travel booking systems in local Crawley companies.
Three years volunteering with the Samaritans — learning to listen.
Active involvement in local clubs and community life across Crawley (On the committee of a local Crawley social sports group).

“I’m a professional. I believe in business, in growth, in making things work. But I’ve never believed those things have to come at the expense of where we live or how we treat each other. Crawley has given me fifty-four years of life. I want to give something back that’s worth having. The Green Party, to me, isn’t about ideology. It’s about the simple idea that every decision — economic, environment  and social — should start by asking what it costs the place and the people around us. That feels like common sense to me. It’s what I’d want from anyone representing me.”

I’ve called Crawley home for six years. In that time, I’ve watched a proud town decline. Those in power caused it—and now expect your trust again. Enough is enough. I’m standing to fix our town centre, make streets safer, sort the roads, and hold them to account.

Thomas has lived in Crawley his whole life. He is committed to strengthening the local community and ensuring that decisions made at council level reflect the needs of residents.
He is a Doctor. His experience includes:
1.Working to get people discharged from hospital by communicating with local authorities, social workers, care services and so on.
2.Witnessing first-hand the challenges faced by our stretched and underfunded public services, and the ways they impact the public.

“I’d never considered standing for political office before, but I’ve been filled with hope by the message of the Green Party. Like a lot of people, I’m drawn to this idea that our politicians should prioritise serving ordinary people, and that positive change should come before negative messaging. I also care deeply about our public services, which haven’t failed, despite being failed by politicians over and over. I want to put your views forward, as best I can, and do what I can to stand up for local voices.”

Lekan Babalola has lived and worked in Crawley for seven years. He is committed to strengthening the local community and ensuring that decisions made at council level reflect the needs of residents.

Professionally, he works as a project manager for a Crawley-based company that has been part of the local area for over 30 years.

He has also been actively involved in the sporting life of Crawley, supporting and participating in a local sports team. He believes that sport plays a vital role in improving both physical and mental health.

“I want to make a meaningful difference in my community in whatever way I can. Over the past three years, the world has changed significantly, and it feels more divided than ever. Seeing injustice both in my local area and across the world has inspired me to step forward and act. I believe that even small changes can have a real impact. If I can help improve people’s lives while working towards this goal, then I will have achieved something truly worthwhile.”

#MakeHopeFeelNormalAgain

I have lived in Crawley for over 40 years and have a wonderful family of 2 beautiful girls and a son who now lives in Japan.

Since I arrived in Crawley my vision was to settle down and retire in the town that has given me the most. During this period I have operated several businesses in the area, most of them had direct contact with the local residents. I understand the pressing needs of residents.

As I am very passionate about the community and the Town I will work very hard to promote positive change needed to improve the community.

In my final 5 working years I have worked in Local Government and understand the operations of the local council and this will speed up any changes required for the community.

I am looking forward to serving the community of Crawley.

Haz (they/them) has lived and worked in Crawley for over 4 years. They are active within the local community and want to ensure that decisions made at council level are representative of the needs of all residents in the community.

Haz is in their final few months of study as an Adult Nurse and is starting their MSc for dual-registration in Mental Health Nursing in September. They work as a front-line homelessness worker at Crawley Open House and have extensive experience and involvement in community work for the homeless outside of this.

“Over several years now I have watched as the country I know and love has descended further into the path of the right wing, and I can’t stand back and watch that happen anymore. I am willing to put the work in to make sure that the communities that our children grow up in are everything they can be. I feel it is my duty to do everything I can to help unite us all and move us towards the common goal of improving living standards for every single one of us, from now, and for always moving forwards. I will always fight for the inclusion and consideration of the most marginalised of us, whether politically or otherwise”.
